**The Crown of the Sky Weaver**

Long ago, when the world was young, the mountains stood bare and brown, envious of the valleys' green beauty. The tallest peak, Kalendra, grew bitter watching birds prefer the lowland forests to her rocky slopes.

One winter night, the Sky Weaver descended from the clouds, her silver hair flowing like starlight. She had grown weary of her eternal work, endlessly spinning weather across the heavens.

"Great mountain," she whispered, "you stand closest to my realm. Will you share my burden? Help me hold the winter's cold, and I shall give you a gift beyond measure."

Kalendra, lonely in her heights, agreed.

The Sky Weaver placed her silver shawl upon Kalendra's summit—a blanket of purest snow. "Wear this crown with pride," she said. "You shall be beautiful year-round, and travelers will journey far to witness your majesty."

When other mountains saw Kalendra's glittering crown, they begged for their own. The Sky Weaver obliged, draping white upon each peak that promised to guard the winter cold.

To this day, mountains wear snow crowns as badges of honor, standing as sentinels between earth and sky, beautiful in their sacred duty.

Moved by Lyra's song, Kael wept. His tears flowed into the cave walls, softening them. When Lyra's final note reached the cave, something miraculous happened—the moistened walls caught the sound and sang it back.

Lyra heard her own voice returning to her and wept with joy. She blessed all caves, canyons, and empty spaces with this gift. "Let no words be truly lost," she declared. "Let every sound have a chance to return."

This is why voices bounce back in empty places, why canyons sing back our shouts, and why lonely words sometimes find their way home. The echo is Kael's gift, remembering what Sound would forget.
